cross-encoder/nli-deberta-v3-base.label: NLI-DeBERTa-v3-base
cross-encoder/nli-deberta-v3-base.description: NLI cross-encoder; entailment probability used as similarity score

examples.text1: DNA is a double-helix molecule with adenine pairing with thymine and guanine pairing with cytosine.
examples.text2: DNA is a double-helix molecule where adenine pairs with cytosine and guanine pairs with thymine — held together by hydrogen bonds.

examples.text1: Worst of all, the Kamakura warriors, resenting the way the Kyoto court referred to them as  Eastern barbarians,  sought refinement in a ruinous taste for  extravagant feasts, rich costumes, and opulent homes.
examples.text2: Sick of being referred to as barbarians, the Kamakura warriors wanted to improve their imagine by doing things to appear more upscale.

examples.text1: The Amazon River carries roughly 20% of the world's fresh water discharge.
examples.text2: The Amazon River carries roughly 20% of the world's fresh water discharge and is the longest river in the world, stretching over 7,000 km.

examples.text1: A boy whacks a man with a sword.
examples.text2: A boy spanks a man with a plastic sword.

examples.text1: Jane, you stay where you are." With a sudden swift movement, Tommy snatched the revolver from Julius's hand, and levelled it at him.
examples.text2: In the blink of an eye, Tommy whisked the gun away from Julius and turned it on him.

examples.text1: you know you don't feel it so strapped
examples.text2: You feel more comfortable.

examples.text1: What law is the Nigerian 419 scam nicknamed after?
examples.text2: These scams involve a complicit Nigerian bank (the laws being set up loosely to allow it) and a scammer who claims to have money he needs to obtain from that bank.

examples.text1: Carbon dioxide is a greenhouse gas that absorbs and re-emits infrared radiation, trapping heat in the atmosphere.
examples.text2: CO₂ is a greenhouse gas that reflects ultraviolet radiation back to Earth, preventing it from escaping into space.

examples.text1: In what building do law students attend classes at KU?
examples.text2: The University of Kansas School of Law was the top law school in the state of Kansas, and 68th nationally, according to the 2014 U.S. News & World Report "Best Graduate Schools" edition.

examples.text1: Hogarth makes no mention of what?
examples.text2: Hogarth gives no evidence of being familiar with the contemporaneous initial concept of the Middle East.

examples.text1: Cross-entropy loss measures the difference between a predicted probability distribution and the true distribution, commonly used for classification tasks.
examples.text2: For classification, cross-entropy loss quantifies how far the model's predicted probabilities diverge from the actual one-hot labels.

examples.text1: How many feet tall was the proposed statue of Schwarzenegger?
examples.text2: In tribute to Schwarzenegger in 2002, Forum Stadtpark, a local cultural association, proposed plans to build a 25-meter (82 ft) tall Terminator statue in a park in central Graz.

examples.text1: What is the only object identified with Neptune's trailing L5 Lagrangian point?
examples.text2: The first and so far only object identified as associated with Neptune's trailing L5 Lagrangian point is 2008 LC18.

examples.text1: The Ottoman Empire caused navigators from which two nations to find another route to the Orient?
examples.text2: The Ottoman Empire stood between the West and the East, thus blocking the land route eastward and forcing Spanish and Portuguese navigators to set sail in search of a new route to the Orient.

examples.text1: What did Whitehead believe was the fundamental purpose of life?
examples.text2: In other words, Whitehead sees life as directed toward the purpose of increasing its own satisfaction.

examples.text1: A red and gray train is going through a tunnel.
examples.text2: A red and gray train travels through a tunnel amongst the trees.

examples.text1: IBM has worked with architects and designers such as Ludwig Mies van der Rohe, I.M. Pei, and Van der Rohe, name one  more.
examples.text2: The company's contributions to architecture and design, which include works by Eero Saarinen, Ludwig Mies van der Rohe, and I.M. Pei, have been recognized.

examples.text1: The SOLID principles are five object-oriented design guidelines intended to make software more maintainable, flexible, and scalable.
examples.text2: SOLID is an acronym for five OOP design principles — Single responsibility, Open/closed, Liskov substitution, Interface segregation, Dependency inversion — that promote clean, maintainable code.

examples.text1: how many lions were on the EIC arms
examples.text2: The supporters were: "Two lions rampant guardant Or, each supporting a banner erect Argent, charged with a cross Gules."

examples.text1: The speed of light in a vacuum is approximately 299,792,458 metres per second.
examples.text2: Light travels through a vacuum at roughly 299,792,458 m/s, commonly approximated as 3 × 10⁸ m/s.

examples.text1: Compound interest is interest calculated on both the principal and the accumulated interest from previous periods.
examples.text2: Unlike simple interest, compound interest grows on the original principal plus any interest already earned, leading to exponential growth over time.

examples.text1: Syria Regime Agrees to Attend Peace Conference
examples.text2: Russia: Syria Agrees to Participate in Conference

examples.text1: Due to his reporting skills, some give Russell credit for doing what?
examples.text2: Some credit Russell with prompting the resignation of the sitting British government through his reporting of the lacklustre condition of British forces deployed in Crimea.

examples.text1: When was the beginning of the Tonian period?
examples.text2: During the beginning of the Tonian period around 1 billion years ago, there was a decrease in Stromatolite diversity, which may indicate the appearance of grazing animals, since stromatolite diversity increased when grazing animals went extinct at the End Permian and End Ordovician extinction events, and decreased shortly after the grazer populations recovered.

examples.text1: How is Raphael portrayed in St. Peter's?
examples.text2: Raphael is represented by a mosaic replica of this last painting, the Transfiguration.

examples.text1: the 5 permanent united nations security council members are britain, china, france, russia and the united states.
examples.text2: p5+1 members are britain, china, france, russia, germany and the united states.

examples.text1: Which station caters to sports fans and enthusiasts?
examples.text2: For sport fans and enthusiasts there is SEN 1116.

examples.text1: Instead, prosecutors dismissed charges and Rucker left the courtroom a free man.
examples.text2: Instead he left the courtroom a free man after authorities dismissed criminal charges.

examples.text1: What was the name of Chopin's doctor?
examples.text2: His death certificate gave the cause as tuberculosis, and his physician, Jean Cruveilhier, was then the leading French authority on this disease.

examples.text1: Who is the current Mayor of London?
examples.text2: The headquarters of the GLA is City Hall, Southwark; the mayor is Boris Johnson.

cross-encoder/stsb-roberta-base.description: STS-B cross-encoder; raw score normalised to [0,1] via sigmoid

examples.text1: Newton's first law of motion states that an object at rest stays at rest, and an object in motion continues in motion at constant velocity unless acted upon by a net external force.
examples.text2: Newton's second law of motion states that force equals mass times acceleration (F = ma), and an object at rest stays at rest unless acted upon by a net external force.

examples.text1: mRNA vaccines deliver messenger RNA that instructs cells to produce a protein triggering immunity.
examples.text2: mRNA vaccines deliver DNA that is first transcribed into mRNA in the cell nucleus, then translated into the target protein to trigger immunity.

examples.text1: How many restaurants is New York home to?
examples.text2: Some 4,000 mobile food vendors licensed by the city, many immigrant-owned, have made Middle Eastern foods such as falafel and kebabs popular examples of modern New York street food.
